title: Modbus Register Value Change Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: IEC 62443 FR 6.2 Continuous monitoring — register-value change tracking is the application-layer evidence of continuous monitoring on Modbus-based automation systems.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 FR 6.2 (Continuous monitoring) is enforced — Splunk UC-14.6.6: Modbus Register Value Change Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-14.6.6' running on sourcetype zeek:modbus_detailed:json and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884)
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json" matched=true
| where func IN ("0x06","6","0x10","16")
| eval reg_key=destination_h."|".unit."|".address
| sort 0 reg_key _time
| streamstats window=2 global=f earliest(response_values) as earlier_resp latest(response_values) as later_resp by reg_key
| where isnotnull(earlier_resp) AND mvjoin(earlier_resp,",")!=mvjoin(later_resp,",")
| table _time source_h destination_h unit address func earlier_resp later_resp request_values
description: Covert changes to holding registers can alter setpoints or interlocks; comparing matched request/response values highlights tampering distinct from normal operator writes.
value: Covert changes to holding registers can alter setpoints or interlocks; comparing matched request/response values highlights tampering distinct from normal operator writes.
implementation: Deploy Zeek with ICSNPP-Modbus on taps near RTUs; ensure `request_values`/`response_values` are indexed. Focus on critical register ranges from asset documentation; schedule correlation with historian trends for validation.
visualization: Table (register_key, old vs new values), Timeline (changes), Line chart (change rate per hour).
knownFalsePositives: Register churn during recipe edits, setpoint sweeps, or batch phase changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json"` (`func`, `address`, `unit`, `request_values`, `response_values`, `matched`, `network_direction`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Deploy Zeek with ICSNPP-Modbus on taps near RTUs; ensure `request_values`/`response_values` are indexed. Focus on critical register ranges from asset documentation; schedule correlation with historian trends for validation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json" matched=true
| where func IN ("0x06","6","0x10","16")
| eval reg_key=destination_h."|".unit."|".address
| sort 0 reg_key _time
| streamstats window=2 global=f earliest(response_values) as earlier_resp latest(response_values) as later_resp by reg_key
| where isnotnull(earlier_resp) AND mvjoin(earlier_resp,",")!=mvjoin(later_resp,",")
| table _time source_h destination_h unit address func earlier_resp later_resp request_values
```

Understanding this SPL

**Modbus Register Value Change Tracking** — Covert changes to holding registers can alter setpoints or interlocks; comparing matched request/response values highlights tampering distinct from normal operator writes.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json"` (`func`, `address`, `unit`, `request_values`, `response_values`, `matched`, `network_direction`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: zeek:modbus_detailed:json. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="zeek:modbus_detailed:json". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where func IN ("0x06","6","0x10","16")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **reg_key** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by reg_key** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(earlier_resp) AND mvjoin(earlier_resp,",")!=mvjoin(later_resp,",")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Modbus Register Value Change Tracking**): table _time source_h destination_h unit address func earlier_resp later_resp request_values

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search for a short window and compare key fields with Zeek or Corelight protocol logs on the sensor for the same flow. Reconcile function codes, endpoints, and time order against a small known-good capture if needed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (register_key, old vs new values), Timeline (changes), Line chart (change rate per hour).
grandmaExplanation: We use deep packet inspection to follow register value changes so we can audit what moved and when in the process.
